description: Guide to common income tax notices, how to read them, and how to respond. All notices since 2019 are served electronically on the e-filing portal (and registered email). Timely, accurate response prevents adverse assessment.
whereToFind: Income Tax Portal → Login → Pending Actions → For Your Action (all notices appear here)

commonNotices.description: Auto-generated intimation after CPC processing. Shows: (a) Return accepted as filed, (b) Demand raised due to CPC adjustments (TDS mismatch, disallowances), or (c) Refund determined.
commonNotices.responseRequired: If demand: Pay within 30 days OR file rectification u/s 154 if error in CPC's computation. If refund: Verify bank account pre-validation.
commonNotices.timeline: Pay demand within 30 days of intimation
commonNotices.importance: HIGH — unaddressed demand leads to recovery

commonNotices.description: Return selected for detailed scrutiny. AO will examine specific issues (income mismatch, large deductions, high refund, industry risk flags). Must respond via e-Proceedings module.
commonNotices.responseRequired: Acknowledge notice. Prepare documentation for specific issues mentioned. File written reply with evidence via e-Proceedings.
commonNotices.timeline: Typically 15-30 days as specified in notice
commonNotices.importance: CRITICAL — non-response leads to ex-parte assessment

commonNotices.description: AO requires information or documents during ongoing assessment. Can ask for books of accounts, investment proofs, loan documents, etc.
commonNotices.responseRequired: Submit requested documents electronically via e-Proceedings within time specified in notice.
commonNotices.timeline: As specified in notice — usually 7-15 days

commonNotices.description: AO proposes to reassess income of a past year (alleging income escaped assessment). Must first give opportunity to respond before issuing re-assessment notice. Finance Act 2021 introduced this show-cause step.
commonNotices.responseRequired: File objections within 15 days explaining why reassessment is not warranted. If AO proceeds despite objections, file return for that year within time given.
commonNotices.timeline: 15 days from 148A notice to file objections
commonNotices.importance: CRITICAL — can result in demands for past years

commonNotices.description: Formal demand notice after assessment order showing tax, interest, and penalty due.
commonNotices.responseRequired: Pay within 30 days OR file appeal to CIT(A) u/s 246A within 30 days if disputing the assessment.

steps.description: Login to incometax.gov.in → Services → e-Proceedings (or Pending Actions). All notices appear here. Download and read carefully.
Note: (a) Section under which issued; (b) AY concerned; (c) Specific issues or income heads questioned; (d) Documents required; (e) Response deadline.
Collect: Form 16/16A, bank statements, investment proofs, property sale documents, business accounts, loan statements — whatever relates to issues raised.
Portal → Services → e-Proceedings → Select the notice → Submit response with written reply (point-by-point addressing each issue) + attach supporting documents.
After response, track on portal. AO may issue questionnaire for further clarification. Respond promptly to all follow-up queries.

commonMistakes: Ignoring notices — AO makes ex-parte assessment after prescribed time, usually unfavorably
Responding after deadline — AO may not consider late response
Providing incomplete documentation — AO makes adverse inference from gaps
Confusing 143(1) intimation with 143(2) scrutiny — 143(1) is usually less serious
Not engaging a CA for complex 143(2) or 148A notices — professional representation helps
consequences: Non-response to 143(2): Best judgment assessment u/s 144 — AO estimates income adversely
Non-payment of 156 demand: Recovery proceedings, attachment of bank accounts u/s 220-232
Ignoring 148A: Re-assessment notice issued without opportunity to object

appealProcess.firstAppeal: Commissioner of Income Tax (Appeals) — CIT(A) u/s 246A within 30 days of order
appealProcess.secondAppeal: Income Tax Appellate Tribunal (ITAT) within 60 days of CIT(A) order
appealProcess.highCourt: High Court on substantial question of law within 120 days of ITAT order
appealProcess.supremeCourt: Supreme Court on special leave petition
